summary: [Fix] Style drop cursor with editor theme

description.text: ## Description This PR adds CSS styling for the drop-cursor (shown when dragging selected text, etc.) ## Changes The editor themes were updated to set the drop cursor color, and the main theme was updated to set the color in dark mode. The CSS attributes were chosen based on the code here: https://github.com/codemirror/view/blob/b7c8f63f079c331fd48e59db84268feb626ae250/src/theme.ts#L125-L138 ## Additional information Currently, the drop cursor is unstyled, and it does not show up against the page in dark mode. Current: PR: Tested on: macOS 15.6

summary: [Fix] Style unfocused selected lint panel items in darkmode

description.text: ## Description This PR styles unfocused selected lint panel items in dark mode to improve accessibility. ## Changes Added a CSS attribute to change the background color of lint panel items when they are selected but unfocused. ## Additional information Previous: PR: Tested on: macOS 15.6

summary: Generate new markdown file based on snippet template

description.text:
### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [ ] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

I sometimes use the template function of Word or LibreOffice: Double click on a template file (eg .ott) and generate a new document based on this template. 

I propose to provide this functionality in Zettlr as well, based on the snippet functionality: If you double click on a markdown file with extension `.tpl.md`, that is not located in Zettlr's snippet directory, a new file is created and Zettlr behaves as if a snippet with the contents of this file would have been inserted via `:` from the snippets assets.

### Example Workflow

Suppose I have to write several documentation files, how to start a program in Windows.

* I create a file `HowToStartX.tpl.md`:

      ```
      How to start ${1:ProgramName}
      ===
      * In Windows 11 click on the Start menu
      * Type `${1:ProgramName}`
      * On the left side of the Start menu "${1:ProgramName}" appears
      * Click on "${1:ProgramName}" to start it
      ```

* I double click on this File
* The file opens, I enter `Excel`
* And save as `HowToStartExcel.md`

### Caveats

Only caveat, I see, would be if you want to edit the existing Zettlr snippets outside the asset manager via Zettlr. Then the new behaviour would generate a new file instead. One could solve this by excluding this dir from the proposed behaviour or by using an other extension.

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Image preview for MD files, that lay on a Windows network share, is still broken

description.text:
### Description

Preview for image files, that are located on a network do not appear in Zettlr: "Image not found: <imagename>". A click in the right corner of the preview opens the correct image.

When exporting (`export.pdf`), only the text of the image is included.

### Reproducing

#### MD on network drive
* create a folder on a network drive in Windows (eg. `\\server\public\test`) and add test.md and test.png.
* open test.md in Zettlr
* drag and drop test.png into test.md in Zettlr: an item `![test.png](test.png)` is added

This image is not displayed in preview.

However: If I copy the whole folder to a local drive, everything works fine.

#### Probably related
* open a local test.md file in Windows
* drag and drop test.png from network share (eg. `\\server\public\test\test.png`) into test.md in Zettlr: an item `![test.png](\\server\public\test\test.png)` is added

This image is not displayed in preview.

However: If I edit to `![test.png](\\\server\public\test\test.png)` (3 backslashes) preview works, but export is still broken.

### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

Chocolatey

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 10

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: fix: Consistent layout of toolbar on Windows

description.text:
The buttons to search for files and toggle the file manager are stacking vertically when the window is shrunk horizontally. This doesn't match the behaviour of those buttons on other platforms where no such stacking occurs.

## Changes
This change updates the CSS so that the layout behaviour is consistent -- no stacking -- across platforms.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9fa85a29-50a7-4348-96e8-b1933a03c364

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bfa996f8-96fa-4b2a-a7ac-a68678b9c195

## Additional information
Noticed while reviewing #5873

The glory for this change belongs to @benniekiss 🙇‍♂️ 

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: Windows 11

summary: [FEAT] Enable a scrolling toolbar on overflow

description.text:
<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:

  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR implements a scrollable area for the toolbar when its content overflows, such as with narrow window widths.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
The toolbar was refactored so that the buttons appear in a scrollable area. The toolbar only becomes scrollable when content overflows. When content overflows, buttons appear on either side of the toolbar indicating the overflow, and they can be clicked to scroll either left or right. When there is no remaining content to one side, the button will be disabled.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

Closes #5022

Behavior:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a1b76f30-f8b0-4c36-a452-c7bfdbd7855e

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 15.6 and Fedora 42 (aarch64)

summary: Improve ToC drag and drop new line handling

description.text:
<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:

  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR improves ToC drag and drop new line handling. It sanitizes new lines in ToC sections to ensure appropriate spacing when moving them.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
the `moveSection` function was refactored to sanitize the section to ensure new lines are handled correctly.

Previously, moving sections would result in an additional new line above the moved section. This was because the section selection extended from the beginning of the section to the end of the last line, but this results in a blank line. By extending the selection to the beginning of the next section, we remove this additional line. 

Secondly, moving sections could result in them being appended to the end of the last line of the previous section, if they were not spaced correctly.

There is one edge case which I cannot seem to figure out, and it can be seen in the video below at about the 20 second mark. But if a section is moved above the previous section, but there is no new line between them, then the previous section will have no new line between it and the next section once it's moved.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->
Previous behavior:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6640edff-18be-4500-9325-c32b28cf72c1

PR behavior:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/579f1c85-1735-4506-af59-c95ee2087dea

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 15.6
